line_evidence.text: -- Sprint 109 Mosco liminf and lower-semicontinuity ledger.
-- This module sharpens the lower-semicontinuity side of the Sprint108
--   lower semicontinuity of the closed quadratic form
--   liminf control of bottom spectral values
"For every weakly convergent bounded-energy lattice sequence, the continuum closed quadratic form is bounded above by the liminf of the finite transfer forms."

strings.blockedBecauseText: Sprint109 records the liminf route but lacks DASHI-native weak compactness, closed-form lower semicontinuity, and lower-edge no-pollution proofs.
strings.closedFormLiminfStatementText: For every weakly convergent bounded-energy lattice sequence, the continuum closed quadratic form is bounded above by the liminf of the finite transfer forms.
strings.closedFormRequirementText: Identify the closed semibounded continuum quadratic form and prove lower semicontinuity on the Mosco liminf domain.
strings.noLowerEdgePollutionRequirementText: Exclude spurious finite-transfer spectral branches converging below the continuum physical lower edge.
strings.nonPromotionBoundaryText: All theorem and Clay promotion flags remain false; this receipt is a lower-bound status ledger, not a continuum mass-gap proof.
strings.spectralLiminfStatementText: The bottom spectral values in the non-vacuum physical sector must satisfy a liminf lower bound compatible with the continuum Hamiltonian lower edge.
strings.spectralProjectionRequirementText: Control spectral projections near the lower edge so approximate finite eigenvectors have continuum physical weak limits.
strings.uniformConstantsRequirementText: Preserve c_transfer, the a-window, and the effective-mass normalization through the liminf spectral passage.

line_evidence.text: "For every vector u in the continuum closed quadratic-form domain with finite energy q(u), choose lattice-scale vectors u_a in the finite carrier Hilbert spaces."
"Energy limsup requirement: limsup_a q_a(u_a) <= q(u), with q_a the renormalized finite transfer quadratic form and q the continuum closed semibounded form."

"Mosco convergence needs both sides: Sprint108 liminf/no-pollution prevents energy loss below the limit, while this recovery limsup side prevents the limit form from being too large and identifies the continuum operator."

strings.energyLimsupText: Energy limsup requirement: limsup_a q_a(u_a) <= q(u), with q_a the renormalized finite transfer quadratic form and q the continuum closed semibounded form.
strings.moscoNecessityText: Mosco convergence needs both sides: Sprint108 liminf/no-pollution prevents energy loss below the limit, while this recovery limsup side prevents the limit form from being too large and identifies the continuum operator.
strings.nonPromotionBoundaryText: Sprint109 records the recovery-sequence side and concrete analytic inputs only; it does not prove Mosco convergence, no-pollution, the transfer lower bound, or the Clay Yang-Mills claim.

--   * finite lattice approximants for each continuum finite-energy vector,

transfer_lower_bound_critical_assembly_l.text: "Sprint109 records the recovery-sequence side and concrete analytic inputs only; it does not prove Mosco convergence, no-pollution, the transfer lower bound, or the Clay Yang-Mills claim."

string_assignments.carrierDensityText: Carrier density boundary: finite lattice physical vectors must be dense in the continuum finite-energy form domain after gauge quotienting, ultraviolet cutoff removal, and finite-volume exhaustion.
string_assignments.continuumFiniteEnergyVectorText: For every vector u in the continuum closed quadratic-form domain with finite energy q(u), choose lattice-scale vectors u_a in the finite carrier Hilbert spaces.
string_assignments.energyLimsupText: Energy limsup requirement: limsup_a q_a(u_a) <= q(u), with q_a the renormalized finite transfer quadratic form and q the continuum closed semibounded form.
string_assignments.finiteApproximantText: The approximants must be built from gauge-compatible finite lattice carriers, projected to the physical sector, and compared through the renormalized embedding/projection maps.
string_assignments.moscoNecessityText: Mosco convergence needs both sides: Sprint108 liminf/no-pollution prevents energy loss below the limit, while this recovery limsup side prevents the limit form from being too large and identifies the continuum operator.
string_assignments.nextAnalyticDensityInputText: Input A: prove a gauge-compatible finite-lattice core dense in the continuum finite-energy form domain, including quotient/physical-sector projection stability.
string_assignments.nextAnalyticEnergyInputText: Input C: prove the renormalized Wilson/Balaban finite forms satisfy the limsup inequality on the recovery maps uniformly over the a-window.
string_assignments.nextAnalyticInterpolationInputText: Input B: construct interpolation and sampling maps between finite carriers and continuum fields with strong norm convergence on the dense core.
string_assignments.nextAnalyticRenormalizationInputText: Input D: fix the coupling, field normalization, volume exhaustion, and embedding conventions so q_a and q are compared in one Hilbert-space identification.
string_assignments.nonPromotionBoundaryText: Sprint109 records the recovery-sequence side and concrete analytic inputs only; it does not prove Mosco convergence, no-pollution, the transfer lower bound, or the Clay Yang-Mills claim.
string_assignments.normConvergenceText: Norm recovery requirement: after the Sprint108 carrier identification, u_a must converge strongly to u in the continuum Hilbert norm.
string_assignments.renormalizationBoundaryText: Renormalization boundary: the raw finite action/form is not the recovery form until coupling, field-strength normalization, volume, and carrier embedding conventions are fixed uniformly in the a-window.

lower_semicontinuity_line_numbers.text: -- Mosco/spectral-liminf package.  It deliberately imports only stable
--     -> spectral liminf for the physical bottom and first threshold
"Sprint109 assembles Mosco lower semicontinuity, recovery, and no-bottom-pollution statuses into a spectral-liminf and gap-transport route."
"Closed-form lower semicontinuity supplies the Mosco liminf inequality on weak finite-energy approximants."

no_bottom_spectrum_pollution_line_number.text: "Sprint109 assembles Mosco lower semicontinuity, recovery, and no-bottom-pollution statuses into a spectral-liminf and gap-transport route."
"No-bottom-pollution excludes spurious nonphysical finite spectral branches below the limiting physical Hamiltonian bottom."
"The Mosco liminf plus no-pollution package gives a spectral liminf boundary for bottom and first-threshold values."

recovery_sequence_line_numbers.text: "Sprint109 assembles Mosco lower semicontinuity, recovery, and no-bottom-pollution statuses into a spectral-liminf and gap-transport route."
"Closed-form lower semicontinuity supplies the Mosco liminf inequality on weak finite-energy approximants."
"Recovery/core density supplies approximants for continuum physical-domain vectors without increasing limiting form energy."

spectral_liminf_assembly_line_numbers.text: -- This module is the high-level assembly lane for the Sprint109
-- Mosco/spectral-liminf package.  It deliberately imports only stable
--     -> spectral liminf for the physical bottom and first threshold

transfer_lower_bound_critical_assembly_l.text: "gap(T(a)) >= c_transfer * m_eff(a) * a transports to a positive continuum physical Hamiltonian gap"
"Spectral liminf and the Sprint108 uniform form ledger are the route to transport the finite transfer lower bound to the continuum gap target."

string_assignments.assemblyStatementText: Sprint109 assembles Mosco lower semicontinuity, recovery, and no-bottom-pollution statuses into a spectral-liminf and gap-transport route.
string_assignments.failClosedBoundaryText: All theorem and Clay-promotion flags remain false until the analytic Sprint109 subpackage receipts are inhabited.
string_assignments.gapTransportStepText: Spectral liminf and the Sprint108 uniform form ledger are the route to transport the finite transfer lower bound to the continuum gap target.
string_assignments.lowerSemicontinuityStepText: Closed-form lower semicontinuity supplies the Mosco liminf inequality on weak finite-energy approximants.
string_assignments.noBottomPollutionStepText: No-bottom-pollution excludes spurious nonphysical finite spectral branches below the limiting physical Hamiltonian bottom.
string_assignments.recoveryStepText: Recovery/core density supplies approximants for continuum physical-domain vectors without increasing limiting form energy.
string_assignments.spectralLiminfStepText: The Mosco liminf plus no-pollution package gives a spectral liminf boundary for bottom and first-threshold values.
string_assignments.targetFormulaText: gap(T(a)) >= c_transfer * m_eff(a) * a transports to a positive continuum physical Hamiltonian gap

no_bottom_spectrum_pollution_line_number.text: -- compactness/tightness ingredient that excludes spurious bottom eigenbranches.
"Spurious bottom eigenbranch exclusion: an eigenvalue branch not converging to the physical vacuum sector may not accumulate below the first continuum threshold."
"The isolated bottom sector supplies the separated vacuum projection; compactness only becomes a no-pollution statement after that sector and the first excited threshold are identified."

recovery_sequence_line_numbers.text: "Every finite low-energy non-vacuum eigenbranch that is tight has a compactly convergent physical subsequence whose limit cannot lie below the isolated continuum bottom sector."
"Compactness requirement: tight bounded-energy branches must admit strongly convergent subsequences in the physical carrier after the finite-to-continuum identification."
"No-collapse-at-zero is recorded as open: non-vacuum eigenvalue sequences are not yet proved to stay uniformly away from zero in the continuum limit."

string_assignments.compactnessText: Compactness requirement: tight bounded-energy branches must admit strongly convergent subsequences in the physical carrier after the finite-to-continuum identification.
string_assignments.finiteSupportLimitationText: Finite transfer positivity and finite support are imported Sprint108 support, but they do not provide tightness, compact extraction, or exclusion of bottom-spectrum pollution.
string_assignments.isolatedBottomSectorRelationText: The isolated bottom sector supplies the separated vacuum projection; compactness only becomes a no-pollution statement after that sector and the first excited threshold are identified.
string_assignments.nextAnalyticRequirementsText: Next requirements: prove tail tightness, compact physical carrier extraction, bottom-sector projection convergence, threshold identification, and a uniform non-vacuum lower bound excluding zero collapse.
string_assignments.noCollapseAtZeroStatusText: No-collapse-at-zero is recorded as open: non-vacuum eigenvalue sequences are not yet proved to stay uniformly away from zero in the continuum limit.
string_assignments.nonPromotionBoundaryText: Sprint109 records compactness obligations only; it does not promote Clay Yang-Mills or close the continuum Hamiltonian mass-gap theorem.
string_assignments.spuriousBranchExclusionText: Spurious bottom eigenbranch exclusion: an eigenvalue branch not converging to the physical vacuum sector may not accumulate below the first continuum threshold.
string_assignments.targetNoPollutionText: Every finite low-energy non-vacuum eigenbranch that is tight has a compactly convergent physical subsequence whose limit cannot lie below the isolated continuum bottom sector.
string_assignments.tightnessText: Tightness requirement: normalized finite low-energy vectors must have uniformly controlled physical tails, gauge-sector leakage, and form energy across 0 < a <= a0.

lower_semicontinuity_line_numbers.text: "Mosco/no-pollution lane remains open: liminf, recovery, and no-pollution witnesses are not inhabited."
"Blocker 1: inhabit Mosco liminf, recovery sequence, and no-pollution lemmas for the transfer lower-bound passage."

string_assignments.criticalEffectText: Critical-path effect: all downstream mass-gap promotion remains blocked until the transfer lower-bound theorem is inhabited.
string_assignments.criticalPathStatusText: Sprint109 critical path records that Sprint108 assembled the transfer lower-bound package, but the target inequality remains a theorem obligation.
string_assignments.hamiltonianPromotionBlockerText: Blocker 6: promote the inhabited transfer lower bound through the Hamiltonian continuum-gap bridge before any Clay status can change.
string_assignments.logGeneratorBlockerText: Blocker 2: prove log-generator spectral mapping for H = -a^-1 log T(a) on a common invariant core.
string_assignments.moscoBlockerText: Blocker 1: inhabit Mosco liminf, recovery sequence, and no-pollution lemmas for the transfer lower-bound passage.
string_assignments.nonPromotionBoundaryText: No continuum Hamiltonian spectral-gap theorem or Clay Yang-Mills promotion is made in Sprint109.
string_assignments.spectralTransportBlockerText: Blocker 4: prove spectral threshold transport from the transfer matrix gap to the continuum Hamiltonian gap.
string_assignments.sprint108ConsumptionText: Sprint109 consumes the canonical Sprint108 transfer lower-bound assembly receipt as typed input.
string_assignments.transferTheoremBlockerText: Blocker 5: inhabit the target theorem gap(T(a)) >= c * m_eff(a) * a from the closed analytic lanes.
string_assignments.uniformFormBlockerText: Blocker 3: prove uniform form coercivity with constants that remain independent of the continuum cutoff.

status_note: The audit reads actual Sprint108 and Sprint109 Agda text. Sprint109 is the fail-closed Mosco closure surface. The route remains fail-closed unless every extracted required Mosco closure theorem flag is explicitly true.

strings.finiteGapToEnergySeparationText: Finite transfer gap input gives only a candidate energy separation after the logarithmic calculus, monotonicity, common-core, and a-normalization inputs are supplied.
strings.localClosureBoundaryText: The physical Hamiltonian theorem is not locally closed because positivity/contractivity, spectral mapping, common-core/domain, and normalization supplies are recorded as requirements rather than proved here.
strings.nonPromotionBoundaryText: Sprint108 records the logarithmic generator calculus bridge only; all theorem and Clay promotion flags remain false.
strings.spectralMappingMonotonicityText: Spectral mapping target: transfer eigenvalues 0 < lambda1 < lambda0 <= 1 map monotonically to energies -a^-1 log lambda1 > -a^-1 log lambda0 for fixed positive a.
strings.transferOperatorText: T(a) is the finite-lattice transfer matrix supplied by the Sprint106 transfer comparison receipt.

line_evidence.text: "Spectral liminf side: bottom spectral values of the finite transfer operators must have continuum lower bounds in the physical sector."
"Finite transfer positivity is imported support, but by itself it gives no uniform continuum lower bound and no no-pollution theorem."
"When Mosco liminf, recovery, no bottom-spectrum pollution, and uniform constants are all supplied, the bridge targets gap(T(a)) >= c * m_eff(a) * a."

strings.bridgeToTargetStatementText: When Mosco liminf, recovery, no bottom-spectrum pollution, and uniform constants are all supplied, the bridge targets gap(T(a)) >= c * m_eff(a) * a.
strings.finiteSupportInsufficientStatementText: Finite transfer positivity is imported support, but by itself it gives no uniform continuum lower bound and no no-pollution theorem.
strings.liminfSpectralStatementText: Spectral liminf side: bottom spectral values of the finite transfer operators must have continuum lower bounds in the physical sector.
strings.lowerSemicontinuityStatementText: Mosco liminf side: weakly convergent finite-energy approximants must not lose energy below the continuum quadratic form.
strings.moscoBridgeStatementText: The finite transfer quadratic forms must converge to the continuum closed semibounded form in the Mosco sense on the physical carrier domain.
strings.noBottomPollutionStatementText: No bottom-spectrum pollution: no spurious finite-transfer eigenvalue branch may converge below the continuum physical Hamiltonian bottom.
strings.openTheoremBoundaryText: The target lower-bound theorem remains open in Sprint108 because this file records required statuses rather than a DASHI-native Mosco/no-pollution proof.
strings.recoverySequenceStatementText: Mosco recovery side: every continuum finite-energy vector needs finite transfer approximants with matching norm and energy limit.

line_evidence.text: -- finite transfer-gap lower bound through the continuum Hamiltonian route.
"finite gap(T(a)) lower bounds transport to a positive continuum physical Hamiltonian spectral gap"
"The lower bound must survive uniformly through a -> 0 after the transfer normalization and Hamiltonian generator identification."

strings.nonPromotionBoundaryText: Sprint108 spectral transport does not promote Clay Yang-Mills and does not infer the continuum Hamiltonian gap from finite transfer positivity alone.
strings.openTransportTheoremBoundaryText: The spectral-gap transport theorem remains open here; the recorded conditions are required boundaries, not a proof.
strings.physicalSpectrumConditionBoundaryText: The limiting operator must be identified with the physical Yang-Mills Hamiltonian on the physical spectrum, including domain and sector restrictions.
strings.spectralTransportTargetFormula: finite gap(T(a)) lower bounds transport to a positive continuum physical Hamiltonian spectral gap
strings.uniformGapSurvivalConditionText: The lower bound must survive uniformly through a -> 0 after the transfer normalization and Hamiltonian generator identification.

line_evidence.text: "The transfer lower-bound theorem remains open because each analytic closure flag in the Sprint108 proof package is still false."

strings.assemblyStatusText: Sprint108 assembles typed Mosco/no-pollution, logarithmic generator, uniform form lower-bound, and spectral transport ledgers.
strings.nonPromotionBoundaryText: No continuum mass-gap or Clay Yang-Mills promotion follows from this Sprint108 assembly.
strings.openTheoremBoundaryText: The transfer lower-bound theorem remains open because each analytic closure flag in the Sprint108 proof package is still false.

line_evidence.text: "For normalized transfer block forms Q_a, the recorded target is Q_a(v) >= c_transfer * m_eff(a) * a * N_a(v) on the physical normalized domain."
"The spectral-gap form of the same ledger is gap(T(a)) >= c_transfer * m_eff(a) * a."
"Blocker: bridge the transfer lower bound to the Hamiltonian spectral gap without losing the uniform positive factor."

strings.hamiltonianBridgeBlockerText: Blocker: bridge the transfer lower bound to the Hamiltonian spectral gap without losing the uniform positive factor.
strings.nonPromotionBoundaryText: No Clay Yang-Mills promotion is made: clayYangMillsPromoted remains false.
strings.normalizationWindowText: Normalization window: 0 < a <= a0, canonical a0 index 16, one normalized transfer block B = 4, and transfer-charge factor c_transfer = 1/64.
strings.notATheoremText: This Sprint108 surface records the uniform form lower-bound ledger; it does not prove the analytic lower-bound theorem.
strings.quadraticFormStatementText: For normalized transfer block forms Q_a, the recorded target is Q_a(v) >= c_transfer * m_eff(a) * a * N_a(v) on the physical normalized domain.
strings.spectralGapStatementText: The spectral-gap form of the same ledger is gap(T(a)) >= c_transfer * m_eff(a) * a.
strings.transferComparisonBlockerText: Blocker: prove the DASHI-native transfer comparison from WC3 effective mass to the transfer-matrix spectral gap with constant c_transfer.
strings.uniformInAStatementText: The constants c_transfer, block size, and WC3 effective-mass independence are recorded uniformly for 0 < a <= a0.
strings.wc3IndependenceText: Sprint106 records desiredMassGapUsed = false, so the WC3 effective-mass lower-bound surface is independent of the desired continuum mass gap.

line_evidence.text: -- path that must be supplied before the transfer lower-bound assembly can be

strings.blockedBecauseText: Sprint109 records the liminf route but lacks DASHI-native weak compactness, closed-form lower semicontinuity, and lower-edge no-pollution proofs.
strings.closedFormLiminfStatementText: For every weakly convergent bounded-energy lattice sequence, the continuum closed quadratic form is bounded above by the liminf of the finite transfer forms.
strings.closedFormRequirementText: Identify the closed semibounded continuum quadratic form and prove lower semicontinuity on the Mosco liminf domain.
strings.domainIdentificationRequirementText: Prove that finite transfer form domains converge to the physical continuum form domain without losing gauge-sector constraints.
strings.noLowerEdgePollutionRequirementText: Exclude spurious finite-transfer spectral branches converging below the continuum physical lower edge.
strings.nonPromotionBoundaryText: All theorem and Clay promotion flags remain false; this receipt is a lower-bound status ledger, not a continuum mass-gap proof.
strings.spectralLiminfStatementText: The bottom spectral values in the non-vacuum physical sector must satisfy a liminf lower bound compatible with the continuum Hamiltonian lower edge.
strings.spectralProjectionRequirementText: Control spectral projections near the lower edge so approximate finite eigenvectors have continuum physical weak limits.
strings.uniformConstantsRequirementText: Preserve c_transfer, the a-window, and the effective-mass normalization through the liminf spectral passage.
strings.weakCompactnessRequirementText: Supply a common physical Hilbert carrier and prove weak compactness for normalized finite-energy transfer-form sequences.

line_evidence.text: "Sprint109 records the recovery-sequence side and concrete analytic inputs only; it does not prove Mosco convergence, no-pollution, the transfer lower bound, or the Clay Yang-Mills claim."
